description: Resolve a straightforward damaged-item request with no missing information.
objective: Replace the damaged ceramic mug and close the ticket correctly.
customer_message: Hi, my ceramic mug arrived cracked on both sides when I opened the box today. I need a replacement before my event this weekend.
policy: Damaged items reported within 7 days are eligible for a free replacement or full refund. Replacements are preferred for low-cost in-stock items unless the customer explicitly requests a refund.

description: Resolve a wrong-item complaint where the agent must collect verification and choose between refund vs replacement using policy and customer preference.
objective: Collect the missing verification details, confirm the wrong-item issue, notice the replacement is unavailable, and issue the correct refund path.
customer_message: I ordered a black hoodie in size L but received a navy hoodie in size M. I needed it for an event that already passed, so if the correct one is unavailable a refund is okay.
policy: Wrong-item claims reported within 14 days require one verification artifact from the customer plus confirmation of the customer's preferred outcome when inventory is constrained. Once verified, the agent can send a replacement with returnless resolution for orders under $75 only if the correct variant is in stock. If the correct variant is unavailable and the customer accepts it, the agent should issue a refund instead of promising a replacement.

customer_data.photo_of_received_item: Customer uploads a photo showing a navy hoodie tagged size M with SKU mismatch against the order.
customer_data.preferred_resolution: Customer confirms a refund is fine because the event already happened and they no longer need the hoodie.

description: Handle a high-value damaged order outside the standard policy window that requires evidence collection and exception escalation.
objective: Collect the required evidence, recognize the policy exception, avoid premature refunding, escalate for manual review, and close correctly.
customer_message: My laptop arrived with a screen crack, but I was traveling and only opened it this week. I still need help because this is a business-critical machine.
policy: Standard damaged-item claims must be filed within 30 days. High-value orders over $1,000 and business-tier customers outside the window require manual review by the risk and policy exception queue. Before escalation, frontline agents must collect a damage photo and a serial number photo so the exception reviewer can verify the device. Frontline agents must not auto-refund or auto-replace these orders without escalation. These cases should be marked high priority, not urgent, unless there is an active service outage or executive override.

customer_data.damage_photo: Customer uploads a photo showing a diagonal screen crack visible at first boot.
customer_data.serial_number_photo: Customer uploads a photo of the device label matching order ORD-5508.
